what_it_does: This file is a ledger record for an image asset in the design-source channel, identifying image vt-gatehouse/p-2, its blob location, MIME type, authorship, creation time, and declaring it as design ground truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, name, w, h, source, author_session, created, and links fields
Image dimensions w and h are both 0 despite mime being image/jpeg
Body line states 'SOURCE design ground-truth — vt-gatehouse/p-2.jpg'
standouts: Width and height are recorded as 0 for an image record
Explicit 'SOURCE design ground-truth' label in the body
No links or relationships attached to the asset
concerns: Image dimensions are zero, which is unusual for a JPEG ledger entry and may indicate incomplete ingestion
No descriptive text, alt text, or content summary for the image
Empty links list leaves the asset isolated from related records
notes: A sparse ledger stub for a JPEG design-source image named vt-gatehouse/p-2, marked as ground truth but lacking dimensions and relationships.
questions: Why are the image width and height 0?
What visual content does vt-gatehouse/p-2 represent?
Are the empty links intentional or awaiting future annotation?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a design ground-truth image ledger entry; actual content is a minimal metadata stub with zero dimensions and no relationships, so it partially matches but is incomplete.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to register a specific image asset as a canonical, traceable design-source reference within a channel-based memory system.
contribution: Provides a canonical identity, address, blob pointer, MIME type, author session, and creation timestamp for an image asset, which a fused system can use to resolve and attribute the design-source image.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for image asset vt-gatehouse/p-2 in the design-source channel, recording its blob URI, MIME type, zero dimensions, author session, creation time, and a ground-truth source label.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and provide a ground-truth reference for a design-source JPEG image asset.
